hero.name: O. Pagya
hero.title: Business Analyst · Product Thinker · AI Enabler
hero.value: I turn ambiguous business problems into scalable product solutions — backed by data, grounded in user needs, and measured by outcomes.
hero.eyebrow: Open to BA · Product Analyst · PM roles

about.heading: I don't just document requirements. I shape product direction.
paragraphs: I'm a Business/Product Analyst with 4 years at IDP Education — one of the world's largest international student placement platforms. My work spans the full product lifecycle: discovery workshops, stakeholder alignment, BRD/FRD documentation, API integration specs, UAT, and post-launch analytics — across multi-country web, mobile, CRM, CMS, and payments platforms.

What sets me apart is the techno-functional bridge I build between business and engineering. I challenge assumptions with data, identify automation opportunities through As-Is/To-Be analysis, and lead AI-driven initiatives that reduce manual workload and scale operational impact. I've designed and shipped custom LLMs, WhatsApp chatbots, and internal AI portals that changed how teams work.

I hold an MBA in Banking Technology from Pondicherry University and a B.E. in Electrical & Electronics Engineering from PSG College of Technology — a combination that gives me both the business rigour and technical fluency to operate at the intersection of product, data, and engineering. I'm CCBA certified and hold multiple IIBA and Product School certifications.
I measure success by outcomes: a 30% conversion lift, $450K/month in revenue leakage stopped, 40% reduction in manual workload. If the number didn't move, the work isn't done.

achievements.title: Conversion Rate Improvement
achievements.desc: Drove a 30% uplift in conversion across IDP's eCommerce and student service funnels through requirements analysis, A/B testing, UX collaboration, and targeted customer-centric enhancements.

achievements.title: Revenue Leakage Mitigated
achievements.desc: Performed root-cause analysis on reconciliation gaps, vendor mismatches, and API integration failures. Drove cross-functional corrective measures that stopped ~$450K/month in silent revenue loss.
achievements.tag: Root Cause Analysis · API · Finance Ops

achievements.title: Manual Workload via AI
achievements.desc: Designed and launched 3 AI-powered solutions — a custom LLM, WhatsApp chatbot, and internal AI portal — that cut manual workload by 40% and significantly improved operational turnaround time.

achievements.title: Journey Completion & CSAT
achievements.desc: Increased journey completion by 25% and boosted customer satisfaction scores by 18% through user research, journey mapping, and cross-functional UX alignment across global platforms.

achievements.title: Reporting Time Saved
achievements.desc: Built and owned BI dashboards covering MRR, funnel analysis, and path exploration — reducing manual reporting effort by 50% and saving 10+ hours per week across Finance and Product teams.

achievements.title: Delivery Timeline Improvement
achievements.desc: Improved delivery timelines by 20% through structured stakeholder workshops (SWOT, MoSCoW, story mapping) that aligned business value with technical feasibility before sprint planning.

caseStudies.title: Stopping $450K/Month in Revenue Leakage
caseStudies.summary: How I identified silent reconciliation failures across IDP's payment ecosystem and led the cross-functional fix that closed the gap.
caseStudies.problem: Finance flagged a growing discrepancy between invoiced amounts and actual payments received across IDP's multi-country platform. The gap had reached ~$450K/month but was being dismissed as 'system noise.' No one had traced it to a root cause.
caseStudies.context: IDP's payments infrastructure spans multiple countries, currencies, and third-party payment providers. Reconciliation between the CRM, payment gateway, and finance systems was partially manual — creating blind spots whenever vendor data or API responses were inconsistent.
caseStudies.role: Lead Business/Product Analyst. I owned the investigation end-to-end — from data analysis and root-cause workshops to requirements definition, cross-functional alignment, and UAT sign-off.

steps.title: Data investigation
steps.desc: Used SQL and BI dashboards to cross-reference payment gateway responses against CRM records. Identified 3 recurring failure patterns: reconciliation gaps, vendor data mismatches, and silent API integration errors.

steps.title: Root cause workshop
steps.desc: Facilitated a cross-functional session with Finance, Engineering, and Payments vendors to map the full transaction flow and pinpoint where data was being lost or misrouted.

steps.title: Stakeholder alignment
steps.desc: Quantified the impact and presented findings to senior leadership. Secured prioritisation on the next sprint cycle.

steps.title: Requirements definition
steps.desc: Wrote BRDs and user stories covering automated reconciliation logic, vendor mismatch alerting, and API error handling with retry mechanisms.

steps.title: UAT & compliance
steps.desc: Designed test scenarios for all failure modes. Validated GDPR alignment for cross-border payment data handling before go-live.

caseStudies.solution: Automated reconciliation logic with real-time discrepancy alerting, vendor mismatch detection, and corrective API retry flows. Paired with a BI dashboard giving Finance full visibility into payment status across all markets.
caseStudies.learnings: Revenue leakage at this scale is almost always a data visibility problem before it's a technical one. SQL fluency and BI ownership gave me the evidence to move stakeholders fast. I've since made reconciliation analysis a standard part of any payments-adjacent discovery.

caseStudies.title: Building AI-Powered Solutions That Cut Manual Work by 40%
caseStudies.summary: How I led the design and launch of a custom LLM, WhatsApp chatbot, and internal AI portal to transform how IDP's teams operate.
caseStudies.problem: IDP's operations teams were spending significant time on repetitive, document-heavy workflows — student query handling, internal knowledge retrieval, and manual data processing. The volume was growing faster than headcount could scale.
caseStudies.context: IDP serves students across multiple countries with complex, document-intensive processes. The business needed intelligent automation that could handle high-volume queries, surface institutional knowledge, and reduce turnaround time — without compromising accuracy or compliance.
caseStudies.role: Lead BA and product owner for all three AI initiatives. I defined the problem space, wrote requirements, coordinated with Engineering and UX, managed stakeholder expectations, and tracked post-launch impact metrics.

steps.title: As-Is process mapping
steps.desc: Documented current workflows for student query handling and internal knowledge retrieval. Identified the highest-volume, highest-friction tasks as automation candidates.

steps.title: Solution scoping
steps.desc: Defined 3 distinct AI use cases — a custom LLM for internal knowledge, a WhatsApp chatbot for student-facing queries, and an AI portal for operational teams. Wrote FRDs for each.

steps.title: Cross-functional build
steps.desc: Collaborated with Engineering on LLM fine-tuning requirements, UX on conversation design, and Compliance on data handling and GDPR alignment.

steps.title: Phased rollout
steps.desc: Launched the WhatsApp chatbot first (highest volume impact), followed by the internal AI portal, then the custom LLM. Monitored deflection rates and turnaround time after each release.

steps.title: Iteration
steps.desc: Used Heap and Hotjar analytics alongside user feedback to identify gaps in chatbot coverage and refine LLM response quality.

caseStudies.solution: Three production AI solutions: a WhatsApp chatbot handling student queries at scale, a custom LLM surfacing institutional knowledge for internal teams, and an AI portal consolidating operational workflows — all GDPR-compliant and integrated into existing systems.
caseStudies.learnings: AI initiatives fail when BAs treat them as tech projects. The real work is process mapping — understanding exactly which human decisions can be automated and which can't. I now start every AI engagement with a workflow audit before touching a single requirement.

caseStudies.title: 30% Conversion Lift on IDP's Student Journey
caseStudies.summary: How user research, funnel analysis, and cross-functional UX alignment turned a stagnant funnel into a measurable growth driver.
caseStudies.problem: IDP's student service funnel was underperforming. Users were dropping off before completing key actions — application submissions, counsellor bookings, and payment flows. Previous UI changes had moved nothing.
caseStudies.context: IDP's website and eCommerce platform serves students across multiple countries navigating complex decisions about studying abroad. The funnel spanned web and mobile, with significant drop-off at multiple stages.
caseStudies.role: Lead BA embedded in the product squad. I owned discovery, requirements, A/B test design, and cross-functional coordination across UX, Engineering, and Data teams.

steps.title: Funnel analysis
steps.desc: Used Google Analytics, Heap, and Hotjar heatmaps to build a step-by-step drop-off map. Identified the exact screens and interactions where users were abandoning.

steps.title: User research
steps.desc: Conducted user research and journey mapping sessions to understand the 'why' behind the data. Uncovered friction points around information clarity, trust signals, and mobile UX gaps.

steps.title: Prioritisation
steps.desc: Ran a MoSCoW workshop with stakeholders to rank findings by impact and effort. Focused the first release on the highest-leverage fixes.

steps.title: A/B testing
steps.desc: Designed and ran A/B tests on key funnel steps. Used results to validate hypotheses before committing to full implementation.

steps.title: Iterative delivery
steps.desc: Shipped improvements across multiple sprints, tracking conversion metrics after each release to confirm impact before proceeding.

caseStudies.solution: A series of targeted, data-validated enhancements across the student journey — improved information architecture, stronger trust signals, mobile UX fixes, and streamlined payment flows — all grounded in user research and A/B test evidence.
caseStudies.learnings: The previous team was optimising the wrong things. Heatmaps and session data told a completely different story than stakeholder assumptions. I now treat analytics tools as the first input to any discovery — not a post-launch afterthought.

caseStudies.title: Delivering Secure API Integrations Across Financial & Healthcare Providers
caseStudies.summary: How I defined integration requirements, ensured GDPR compliance, and enabled IDP to onboard banking, payments, and insurance services across global markets.
caseStudies.problem: IDP needed to expand its service offering by integrating with financial and healthcare providers — enabling students to access banking, insurance, and payment services directly through the platform. The challenge: each provider had different API contracts, data formats, and compliance requirements across multiple countries.
caseStudies.context: IDP operates across 50+ countries. Any integration touching financial or health data requires strict GDPR alignment, country-specific compliance checks, and robust error handling. Engineering teams needed precise, testable specifications — not vague business requirements.
caseStudies.role: Lead BA for all financial and healthcare API integrations. I owned the requirements, integration mapping, compliance review, and UAT coordination from discovery through go-live.

steps.title: Provider discovery
steps.desc: Mapped each provider's API documentation, data schemas, and authentication flows. Identified gaps between provider capabilities and IDP's platform requirements.

steps.title: Integration requirements
steps.desc: Wrote detailed FRDs covering request/response schemas, error handling, retry logic, timeout behaviour, and data transformation rules for each integration.

steps.title: Compliance review
steps.desc: Worked with Legal and Compliance to validate GDPR alignment for each data type — particularly PII handling, consent flows, and cross-border data transfer rules.

steps.title: UAT design
steps.desc: Created test scenarios covering happy paths, edge cases, and failure modes. Coordinated UAT execution with Engineering, QA, and provider teams.

steps.title: Go-live & monitoring
steps.desc: Defined post-launch monitoring criteria and built BI alerts for integration failure rates. Established a feedback loop for ongoing provider issues.

caseStudies.solution: A suite of production-ready API integrations with financial and healthcare providers — fully documented, GDPR-compliant, and monitored — enabling IDP to offer banking, payments, and insurance services to students across global markets.
caseStudies.learnings: API integration work lives or dies on the quality of the requirements. Vague specs create ambiguity that surfaces as bugs in UAT — or worse, in production. I now treat integration FRDs as contracts: every field, every error code, every edge case documented before a single line of code is written.

experience.summary: Embedded across IDP's core product squads, owning requirements end-to-end for multi-country platforms spanning Student Services, CRM, CMS, Payments, and Partner Integrations. Act as the techno-functional bridge between business stakeholders and engineering teams globally.

Improved conversion rates by 30% through A/B testing, UX collaboration, and customer-centric enhancements
Mitigated ~$450K/month in revenue leakage via root-cause analysis on reconciliation gaps and API failures
Designed and launched 3 AI solutions (custom LLM, WhatsApp chatbot, AI portal) — reduced manual workload by 40%
Increased journey completion by 25% and customer satisfaction by 18% through user research and journey mapping
Built BI dashboards (MRR, funnel, path analysis) cutting manual reporting by 50% and saving 10+ hours/week
Delivered GDPR-compliant API integrations with financial and healthcare providers across global markets
Improved delivery timelines by 20% through structured stakeholder workshops (SWOT, MoSCoW, story mapping)
Managed product ideation, market analysis, stakeholder communication, and roadmap planning for IDP Website and eCommerce payments

education.coursework: Product Analysis, Lean & Six Sigma, Data Analytics, Power BI

certifications.name: CCBA – Certification of Capability in Business Analysis
Agile Product Owner & Data-Driven Product Management

pmExercises.title: Define a Product Vision & Strategy
pmExercises.description: Pick any product you use daily. Write a 1-page product vision, identify the target user segment, define the core value proposition, and outline a 6-month roadmap with 3 key bets.
pmExercises.tip: Start with 'Who is this for?' before 'What should we build?'

pmExercises.title: MoSCoW Prioritisation Exercise
pmExercises.description: Take a backlog of 15–20 features for a hypothetical product (e.g. a student visa app). Categorise each as Must Have, Should Have, Could Have, or Won't Have. Justify each decision with a business rationale.
pmExercises.tip: If everything is a Must Have, nothing is. Force yourself to put at least 40% in Should/Could.

pmExercises.title: User Journey Mapping
pmExercises.description: Map the end-to-end journey of a student applying to study abroad — from first awareness to visa approval. Identify touchpoints, emotions, pain points, and opportunities at each stage.
pmExercises.framework: Journey Map (Stages → Actions → Emotions → Pain Points → Opportunities)
pmExercises.tip: Interview at least 2 real users before drawing the map. Assumptions kill journey maps.

pmExercises.title: Define a Metrics Framework (HEART / AARRR)
pmExercises.description: Choose a product (e.g. a food delivery app). Define its North Star Metric, then build a full AARRR funnel (Acquisition, Activation, Retention, Referral, Revenue) with specific KPIs and measurement methods for each stage.
pmExercises.deliverable: Metrics tree with KPIs, targets, and data sources
pmExercises.tip: The North Star Metric should capture value delivered to users — not just revenue.

pmExercises.title: Root Cause Analysis with 5 Whys
pmExercises.description: Take a real product failure scenario (e.g. 'checkout abandonment increased by 20% last week'). Apply the 5 Whys technique to trace from symptom to root cause. Then write a hypothesis and proposed solution.
pmExercises.deliverable: 5 Whys chain + fishbone diagram + solution hypothesis
pmExercises.tip: Don't stop at the first 'why' that feels satisfying. Push to layer 4 or 5.

pmExercises.title: Market Sizing & Estimation
pmExercises.description: Estimate the total addressable market for a 'WhatsApp for enterprise compliance' product in India. Use both top-down (TAM/SAM/SOM) and bottom-up approaches. Show your assumptions clearly.
pmExercises.deliverable: Market sizing model with assumptions documented
pmExercises.tip: Interviewers care more about your reasoning than the final number. Show your work.

pmExercises.title: Design a Feature from Scratch
pmExercises.description: Design a 'smart document checklist' feature for a student visa application platform. Write the user story, acceptance criteria, edge cases, and sketch a basic wireframe. Include how you'd measure success.
pmExercises.framework: User Story → Acceptance Criteria → Wireframe → Success Metrics
pmExercises.deliverable: User story + AC + wireframe sketch + metrics definition
pmExercises.tip: Write the acceptance criteria before the wireframe. It forces clarity on what 'done' means.

pmExercises.title: Stakeholder Mapping & Communication Plan
pmExercises.description: For a CRM implementation project, identify 8–10 stakeholders. Map them on a Power/Interest grid. Then write a tailored communication plan for each quadrant — what to share, how often, and in what format.
pmExercises.deliverable: Stakeholder map + RACI matrix + communication plan
pmExercises.tip: High power, low interest stakeholders are the most dangerous to ignore. Keep them satisfied proactively.

contact.intro: I'm open to Business Analyst, Product Analyst, and Product Manager roles — particularly in EdTech, FinTech, or digital transformation. If you're building something complex and need someone who can own the problem space end-to-end, let's talk.
